source.title: From prediction error to incentive salience: mesolimbic computation of reward motivation

confidence_rationale: Berridge's incentive salience framework provides the strongest neuroscientific foundation for MECH-216. The mechanism described -- learned CS-UCS associations dynamically generating wanting at re-encounter, modulated by physiological state -- maps directly onto E1 schema activation seeding VALENCE_WANTING. High source quality (canonical review by the field's originator), strong mapping fidelity (the CS-to-wanting transformation is precisely what MECH-216 proposes in latent space), moderate transfer risk (biological dopamine circuits vs. computational E1 schema readout).
failure_signatures: Berridge emphasises that wanting can be generated for stimuli never previously liked -- MECH-216 does not model this dissociation (wanting is always grounded in resource benefit).
The biological mechanism requires mesolimbic dopamine as the wanting-generation substrate; REE has no dopaminergic analog -- serotonin module handles a different function.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Mesolimbic dopamine systems dynamically compute incentive salience by combining learned Pavlovian CS-UCS associations with current neurobiological state at the moment of cue re-encounter, generating wanting independently of hedonic liking or cognitive expectation.
mapping.ree_translation: E1 schema activations that reliably predict resource encounters (learned CS-UCS associations) seed VALENCE_WANTING at the current z_world position (incentive salience attribution at cue re-encounter). The drive_level / physiological state modulation (kappa parameter) maps to SD-012 homeostatic drive scaling.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Berridge's wanting is mediated by mesolimbic dopamine; REE's wanting is a field value in the residue field updated by the serotonin module. The biological wanting can dissociate from liking and from prediction -- REE's wanting is currently tied to benefit/resource proximity. The kappa-modulation (state-dependent gain) maps loosely to drive_level but REE does not yet implement moment-by-moment recomputation of wanting at re-encounter.
mapping.source_context: Review of rodent and human neuroscience on dopamine, incentive salience, and Pavlovian-to-instrumental transfer.

confidence_components.notes: Canonical review by the field originator. High-quality empirical grounding (salt appetite, amphetamine sensitisation, lesion studies). Mapping fidelity docked because MECH-216 does not replicate the wanting/liking dissociation -- E1 schema wanting is always resource-grounded.
